spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (drawElement) with runtime script (~4283 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 7 buttons, 0 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (canvas, card, custom, dark, directly, including, mode, render, variables).

edge_cases.rationale: Page references 6 edge case / failure / fallback hits and the control surface has room to exercise distinct branches, so the happy path is not the only one shown.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("HTML-in-canvas enables customizing the rendering of html using canvas with three new primitives: an attribute to opt-in canvas elements (layoutsubtree), methods to draw child elements (2d: drawElementImage, webgl: texElementImage2D, webgpu:") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/whatwg/html/pull/11588 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~4283 chars of script across 7 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 5 pass / 1 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
